---
title: "About LASER"
page-layout: full
---

## About LASER

**LASER (Laboratory for Autonomous Systems Engineering Research)** is a research group in the Department of Chemical Engineering at Sunchon National University, Republic of Korea.

Engineering complex process systems — designing experiments, building predictive models, making operational decisions — traditionally demands sustained expert involvement at every step. LASER's research program is built around a single question: how much of that involvement can be replaced by principled, verifiable computation? We address this through methods grounded in sequential experimental design, hybrid surrogate modeling, uncertainty-aware decision-making, and systematic validation, applied across process, electrochemical, and bioprocess domains.

---

## Contact {#contact}

### Location

Department of Chemical Engineering
Sunchon National University
255 Jungang-ro, Suncheon-si, Jeollanam-do 57922
Republic of Korea

### Contact Information

- **Email**: khryu@scnu.ac.kr
- **Office**: D3-305
- **Phone**: 061-750-3588

---

## Join Our Lab {#join-our-lab}

We are always looking for motivated people to join the team. Below are current openings.

### Open Positions

#### Researchers & Postdoctoral Fellows

We welcome inquiries from researchers and postdoctoral candidates with a background in one or more of the following areas:

- Process systems engineering, systems modeling, or computational engineering
- Optimization, optimal control, or decision-making under uncertainty
- Machine learning, Bayesian methods, or data-driven modeling
- Experimental design or active learning in engineering contexts

Candidates are encouraged to reach out directly with a CV and a brief description of their research interests.

#### Graduate Students (M.S. / Ph.D.)

We accept applications from students interested in building research skills in:

- Autonomous learning and experimental design (DOE, BO, active learning)
- Hybrid process modeling and surrogate-based optimization
- Process monitoring, fault detection, and operational decision-making

No prior research experience is required — curiosity and a willingness to engage with both computation and engineering fundamentals matter more.

### How to Apply

Please send the following to **khryu@scnu.ac.kr**:

- CV or resume
- Transcript (for graduate applicants)
- Brief statement of research interests (1 page or less)
- Any relevant publications, reports, or research experience (if available)

---

::: {.callout-note}
## Prospective Graduate Students
For information about graduate admissions at Sunchon National University, please visit the [university website](https://www.scnu.ac.kr).
:::
